subject: Welcome to my portfolio 👋
preview: Thanks for stopping by — here's how to reach me and what I'm working on.
body:
Hi there,

Thanks for exploring this macOS Tahoe portfolio. Every app here is real: live GitHub data, live weather, a working file browser over the actual repo, and Mail you're reading right now.

I'm a frontend developer and UI/UX designer who sweats the details — Liquid Glass, motion, accessibility, the works. If you'd like to build something together, just hit reply and I'll get back to you within a day.

— Daniel
mail@danielprior.dk · danielprior.dev

subject: [daprior/danielprior-macos] New star milestone reached
preview: Your repository danielprior-macos just passed 180 stars this week.
body:
Nice work!

daprior/danielprior-macos crossed 180 stars this week, with 14 new stargazers and 3 new forks. Here's a recap of recent activity:

• 7 pushes to main
• 2 pull requests merged
• 1 new issue opened

Keep building — your community is growing.

The GitHub Team

subject: Senior Frontend Engineer — Northwind (intro)
preview: Came across your portfolio and the Liquid Glass work blew me away.
body:
Hi Daniel,

I'm a technical recruiter at Northwind, and I came across your macOS portfolio — the attention to detail in the Liquid Glass interfaces genuinely stopped me in my tracks.

We're hiring a Senior Frontend Engineer to lead the design-systems track on our product team. It's a remote-first role (we're CET-friendly), React/Next.js/TypeScript, with real ownership over the component library.

Would you be open to a 20-minute intro call next week? Happy to work around your schedule.

Best,
Priya Nair
Talent · Northwind

subject: You appeared in 27 searches this week
preview: Recruiters and engineers found your profile — see who's looking.
body:
Hi Daniel,

Your profile showed up in 27 searches this week, up 40% from last week. Most searchers work in Software Development and Design, at companies like Northwind, Figma, and Vercel.

• 9 profile views
• 27 search appearances
• 4 new connection requests

Update your headline to keep showing up in the right searches.

The LinkedIn Team

subject: Re: Freelance — landing page refresh
preview: Looks great. One small tweak on the hero animation and we're good to ship.
body:
Hey Daniel,

Reviewed the staging build — it looks fantastic. The team loved the scroll-driven hero.

One small tweak: can we slow the hero animation by ~150ms so the headline lands before the gradient settles? Other than that, we're ready to ship Friday.

Invoice approved on our end — finance will process this week.

Cheers,
Marcus
Studio Atlas

subject: Frontend Focus #642 — Container queries, View Transitions & more
preview: This week: the State of CSS 2026, View Transitions in the wild, and a deep dive on signals.
body:
Frontend Focus #642

In this issue:

• The State of CSS 2026 results are in — container queries hit 90% adoption
• View Transitions API: production patterns from three large sites
• A practical guide to fine-grained reactivity with signals
• 5 new open-source UI libraries worth a look
• Job board: 18 new frontend roles

Read the full issue online. You're receiving this because you subscribed at frontendfoc.us.
